county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lamb County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Littlefield. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Littlefield. South Plains county organized 1908. Lamb County Historical Commission. Farming (cotton, grain). Check Bexar for pre-organization records. XIT Ranch territory (southern portion).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Lamb County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Littlefield holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Lamb County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Lamb County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1876. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Lamb County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1876. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Lamb County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Lamb County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Lamb County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Lamb County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lampasas County formed 1856 from Bell, Travis. County seat is Lampasas. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lampasas. Central Texas county. Lampasas County Museum. Mineral springs resort heritage. Horrell-Higgins feud records. Check Bell and Travis for pre-1856 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Lampasas County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in Lampasas holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Lampasas County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1856. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1856. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Lampasas County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1856 to 1950 for Lampasas County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Lampasas County Area (various tribes)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Lampasas County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Lampasas County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Lampasas County from 1856. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lavaca County formed 1846 from Colorado, Gonzales, Jackson, Victoria. County seat is Hallettsville. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hallettsville. South-central Texas county with strong Czech and German communities. Lavaca County Historical Commission. Czech Heritage Museum in Temple (regional). Painted churches heritage. Check Colorado, Gonzales, Jackson, Victoria for pre-1846 records. Hallettsville Kolache Fest reflects Czech heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Lavaca County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Hallettsville holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Lavaca County Deed Records -- County Clerk

source_profiles.source_name: Lavaca County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Lavaca County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Lavaca County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Lavaca County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Lavaca County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Lavaca County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Lavaca County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Lavaca County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Lavaca County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Lee County formed 1874 from Bastrop, Burleson, Washington. County seat is Giddings. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Giddings. Central Texas county. Lee County Heritage Center. Czech and German communities (Lexington, Dime Box area). Check Bastrop, Burleson, Washington for pre-1874 records. Wendish (Sorbian) settlers at Serbin (St. Paul Lutheran Church records from 1854).

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Liberty County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Liberty). County seat is Liberty. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Liberty. Original Texas county — one of the oldest settled areas. Sam Houston Regional Library and Research Center in Liberty is a major repository. Atascosito Road heritage. Mexican-era land grants. Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC. Significant African American community. Liberty County Historical Commission.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Liberty County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Liberty holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Liberty County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Liberty County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in George West. South Texas county. Live Oak County Historical Commission. Ranching heritage. Check Nueces and San Patricio for pre-1856 records. Choke Canyon Reservoir area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Llano County formed 1856 from Bexar, Gillespie. County seat is Llano. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Llano. Hill Country county. Llano County Historical Museum. Granite quarrying (Llano County supplied granite for the State Capitol). German settlement heritage. Check Bexar and Gillespie for pre-1856 records. Enchanted Rock State Natural Area.
